In the second part, we have covered the components of the Lang chain. We discussed the components one by one like the components of models, prompts, chains etc.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: But to understand the tools, we will have to talk about LLMs first. So LLMs, as you know, are very powerful natural language processing systems. If I ask you what is the biggest quality of LLMs, what is the biggest power of LLMs, then you will probably say two things. First, you will say that LLMs have reasoning capability, which means that if you provide a question to LLMs, then LLM understands that question and breaks down how to answer that question.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: which means that if you provide a question to LLMs, then LLM understands that question and breaks down how to answer that question. So basically, the LLM can think. Right? This is the first core capability of an LLM. The second one is language generation. Once LLMs understand how to answer a given question, then LLMs generate answers word by word.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: Once LLMs understand how to answer a given question, then LLMs generate answers word by word. Which means in a way we can say that LLMs have the capability to speak. So in a nutshell, today's LLMs have two core capabilities. First, to think. Second, to speak. But that's it. Apart from this, LLMs have no other power.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: First, to think. Second, to speak. But that's it. Apart from this, LLMs have no other power. For example, if I ask LLMs what is the best way to go to Mumbai from Delhi, then they will think and tell you the answer. That flight is one option, train is one option, bus is another option. But if I tell them to book my train, can LLMs book it for you?

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: That flight is one option, train is one option, bus is another option. But if I tell them to book my train, can LLMs book it for you? The answer is no. LLMs don't have the power to perform tasks for you. So in a way you can say that LLMs are like a human body that has the capability to think and speak, but that human body doesn't have hands or legs.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: So in a way you can say that LLMs are like a human body that has the capability to think and speak, but that human body doesn't have hands or legs. Which means that it can't execute tasks on its own. So there are a lot of tasks that computer systems can do today, but LLMs can't. For example, fetching live weather data. LLMs can't. Reliability of maths. LLMs can perform basic addition and subtraction.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: For example, fetching live weather data. LLMs can't. Reliability of maths. LLMs can perform basic addition and subtraction. But if you give it a complex maths problem, there is a good chance that the answer won't be reliable. Because LLMs have not learned how to solve maths. They have learned language generation. LLMs can't call external APIs. If you say that tweet on Twitter on my behalf, then LLMs can't.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: So as I said, you can see LLMs like a human body that has the capability to think and speak, but that human body doesn't have hands or legs. So what are tools? Tools are a mechanism through which you can give LLMs hands or legs. Meaning, you can create a tool to perform any given task and connect that tool with your LLMs.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: So what are tools? Tools are a mechanism through which you can give LLMs hands or legs. Meaning, you can create a tool to perform any given task and connect that tool with your LLMs. As soon as you perform this connection, as soon as that task is given to LLMs, then LLMs will execute that task with the help of that tool. Technically speaking, tools are nothing but functions.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: then LLMs will execute that task with the help of that tool. Technically speaking, tools are nothing but functions. So this is the logic of executing a task. Now what you do is you package this function in such a way that it can interact with LLMs. So for example, you created a function that can perform booking on IRCTC's website.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: So for example, you created a function that can perform booking on IRCTC's website. So now you can see that LLMs are able to communicate with the LLMs. So now as soon as I ask LLMs to tell me about the trains from Delhi to Mumbai, then LLMs will tell me.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: So now you can see that LLMs are able to communicate with the LLMs. So now as soon as I ask LLMs to tell me about the trains from Delhi to Mumbai, then LLMs will tell me. Now as soon as I ask LLMs to book a train for me, then since LLMs have access to this function, then LLMs will book a ticket for you with the help of this function.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: Now as soon as I ask LLMs to book a train for me, then since LLMs have access to this function, then LLMs will book a ticket for you with the help of this function. And that is the power of tools. The more tools you add to LLMs, the more tasks your LLMs can perform. Here a very simple definition is written. A tool is just a Python function that is packaged in a way that LLMs can understand and call when needed.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: Here a very simple definition is written. A tool is just a Python function that is packaged in a way that LLMs can understand and call when needed. Now if we discuss about tools a little more, then you get two types of tools in Lankchain.

For example, searching on Google, internet, Wikipedia, command line tools.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: See what is written here. So agent is a system that has two capabilities. First, it can think, reason and second, it can take action. Meaning it can perform tasks about the things that it has thought.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
chunks.text: First, it can think, reason and second, it can take action. Meaning it can perform tasks about the things that it has thought. So in a nutshell, agents have two capabilities. Now this reasoning and decision making part of agent comes from LLM.

chunks.video_name: Video_18_Tools in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 16 ｜ CampusX.mp3
